name: Flourless Chocolate Mocha Cake
recipeInstructions:
chopped, melted plus 3 ounces melted, for garnish
Preheat oven to 425. Line a 9 inch round cake pan with
parchment paper. Mix together chocolate and butter in top
of double boiler over simmering water and stir often until
melted. Remove from heat. Place eggs in mixing bowl over
warm tap water until lukewarm. Beat eggs with electric
mixer until light color and tripled in volume - about 3
minutes at medium speed and then on high for 5 minutes.
Add half the beaten eggs and ground espresso beans to chocolate
mixture. Fold in gently. Then, add remaining eggs and fold
in just until streaks remain. Do not overmix.
Pour into cake pan and place in large pan with boiling water coming up sides of cake pan about halfway. Bake for 5 minutes. Cover cake pan with foil and bake another 10 minutes. Set aside to cool on rack for 45 minutes. Chill for 6 hours or overnight. To remove cake from pan, place cake pan in oven preheated to 350 degrees for a minute or two. Remove and run a sharp knife around inside edge to loosen. Invert onto a platter.
Beat whipping cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until peaks form. Pile on top of chocolate cake and spread out to edges. Chill for 10 minutes to set. Arrange toasted pecan halves on top of cream in a circle. Drizzle melted chocolate over center in zig-zag, free-form pattern. Chill until serving time.
